content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

The change set is incomplete and likely incorrect for the requested backend-default migration: it does not touch default-resolution code and introduces committed run-state artifacts that encode a noncompliant final-review reviewer pair.

## Amendments

1. High: The current patch does not change backend-default implementation logic at all—it only updates live run state files, so the requested default-behavior changes are not actually implemented in code (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs` and `src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs` are unchanged). The diff adds only `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/journal.ndjson:35-43` and `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/run.json:16-73`.
   Rationale: Acceptance criteria require code-path changes for implementer/reviewer defaults; this patch updates only serialized runtime artifacts.
2. High: The persisted final-review snapshot in tracked run state now hardcodes both reviewers as Codex backends (`gpt-5.4-xhigh` and `gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh`) in `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/run.json:19-27` and again in `:52-63`, while journal entries show the same pair during proposal (`.ralph-burning/projects/utm/journal.ndjson:42-43`). This conflicts with the required default reviewer pair (Codex xhigh + Opus 4.6) and can cause resumed runs to inherit an unintended noncompliant reviewer set from checked-in state.
   Rationale: Persisted stage-resolution snapshots are authoritative for resume/drift behavior; committing this state can reintroduce default-regression behavior and violate the bead’s acceptance criteria.
